case_laws.title: Mst. Sadia v. Muhammad Irfan
case_laws.summary_en: Landmark case on Khula (wife's right to dissolution of marriage). The Supreme Court held that a wife's right to Khula is absolute and the court cannot refuse Khula if the wife has given up her financial rights including dower. The wife need not prove specific grounds for seeking dissolution.
case_laws.summary_ur: خلع پر تاریخی فیصلہ۔ سپریم کورٹ نے فیصلہ دیا کہ بیوی کا خلع کا حق مطلق ہے۔
case_laws.headnotes: Khula - Wife's absolute right - Dissolution of Muslim Marriages Act 1939 - No specific grounds required - Surrender of dower
case_laws.relevant_statutes: ["Dissolution of Muslim Marriages Act", "Muslim Family Laws Ordinance", "West Pakistan Family Courts Act"]

case_laws.title: Muhammad Akram v. Mst. Fatima
case_laws.summary_en: Case regarding child custody (Hizanat) rights. The court upheld the mother's right to custody of minor children, holding that the welfare of the child is the paramount consideration. The mother's right to custody of a boy continues until age 7 and for a girl until puberty.
case_laws.summary_ur: بچوں کی حضانت کے حقوق سے متعلق مقدمہ۔
case_laws.headnotes: Child custody - Hizanat - Mother's preferential right - Welfare of child paramount - Guardian and Wards Act 1890
case_laws.relevant_statutes: ["Guardian and Wards Act", "West Pakistan Family Courts Act", "Muslim Family Laws Ordinance"]

case_laws.title: Mst. Nazia v. Syed Imran
case_laws.summary_en: Case on maintenance (Nafqa) rights of wife and children. The court held that a husband is legally obligated to provide maintenance to his wife during marriage and iddat period, and to his minor children. The maintenance amount should be determined based on the husband's financial capacity.
case_laws.summary_ur: بیوی اور بچوں کے نفقے کے حقوق سے متعلق مقدمہ۔
case_laws.headnotes: Maintenance - Nafqa - Husband's obligation - Wife's right during marriage and iddat - Children's maintenance - Section 9 MFLO
case_laws.relevant_statutes: ["Muslim Family Laws Ordinance", "West Pakistan Family Courts Act"]

case_laws.title: Mst. Rukhsana v. Tariq Mehmood
case_laws.summary_en: Case concerning the enforceability of dower (Mahr). The court held that dower is a debt owed by the husband to the wife and ranks as a preferential debt. Prompt dower is payable on demand and deferred dower becomes payable on dissolution of marriage or death of husband.
case_laws.summary_ur: مہر کے نفاذ سے متعلق مقدمہ۔ عدالت نے فیصلہ دیا کہ مہر شوہر پر بیوی کا قرض ہے۔
case_laws.headnotes: Dower - Mahr - Debt owed by husband - Prompt dower on demand - Deferred dower on dissolution - Preferential debt
case_laws.relevant_statutes: ["Muslim Family Laws Ordinance", "West Pakistan Family Courts Act"]

case_laws.title: Mst. Amina Bibi v. Muhammad Yousaf
case_laws.summary_en: Case on the validity of second marriage without permission of Arbitration Council under Section 6 MFLO. The court held that a second marriage contracted without Arbitration Council permission is not void but irregular. However, the first wife is entitled to receive entire dower amount immediately.
case_laws.summary_ur: ثالثی کونسل کی اجازت کے بغیر دوسری شادی کی قانونی حیثیت سے متعلق مقدمہ۔
case_laws.headnotes: Polygamy - Section 6 MFLO - Arbitration Council permission - Second marriage not void but irregular - First wife's dower rights

case_laws.title: Muhammad Ashraf v. Mst. Zubaida
case_laws.summary_en: Supreme Court case on visitation rights of a non-custodial parent. The court held that the non-custodial parent has an inherent right to meet and interact with the child. Denial of visitation rights is harmful to the child's emotional development. Courts must facilitate meaningful contact.
case_laws.summary_ur: غیر حاضن والدین کے ملاقاتی حقوق سے متعلق سپریم کورٹ کا مقدمہ۔
case_laws.headnotes: Visitation rights - Non-custodial parent - Child welfare - Meaningful contact - Guardian and Wards Act
case_laws.relevant_statutes: ["Guardian and Wards Act", "West Pakistan Family Courts Act"]

case_laws.title: Mst. Saira v. Abdul Qadir
case_laws.summary_en: Case on domestic violence under the Domestic Violence Act. The court issued a protection order restraining the husband from committing acts of violence. The court held that emotional and economic abuse also constitute domestic violence, not just physical violence.
case_laws.summary_ur: گھریلو تشدد ایکٹ کے تحت مقدمہ۔ عدالت نے تحفظ کا حکم جاری کیا۔
case_laws.headnotes: Domestic violence - Protection order - Physical, emotional and economic abuse - Domestic Violence Act 2012

case_laws.title: Muhammad Farooq v. Mst. Nasreen
case_laws.summary_en: Case on the enforceability of Talaq notice under Section 7 MFLO. The court held that Talaq pronounced without giving notice to the Chairman of Union Council is not effective. The 90-day reconciliation period is mandatory and Talaq becomes effective only after expiry of this period.
case_laws.summary_ur: دفعہ 7 ایم ایف ایل او کے تحت طلاق نوٹس کے نفاذ سے متعلق مقدمہ۔
case_laws.headnotes: Talaq - Section 7 MFLO - Notice to Chairman mandatory - 90 days reconciliation - Effective after expiry

case_laws.title: Mst. Farzana v. Shahid Ali
case_laws.summary_en: Supreme Court ruled on inheritance rights of women under Islamic law. The court affirmed that depriving female heirs of their Quranic share of inheritance is illegal and constitutes an offense under the Prevention of Anti-Women Practices Act 2011. Customary practices cannot override Islamic inheritance law.
case_laws.summary_ur: اسلامی قانون کے تحت خواتین کے وراثت کے حقوق پر سپریم کورٹ کا فیصلہ۔
case_laws.headnotes: Women's inheritance - Quranic share - Prevention of Anti-Women Practices Act - Customary practices void - Islamic law paramount
case_laws.relevant_statutes: ["Prevention of Anti-Women Practices Act", "Muslim Personal Law (Shariat) Application Act"]

case_laws.title: Mst. Shagufta v. Nadeem Ahmed
case_laws.summary_en: Case on the jurisdiction of Family Courts under WPFCA. The court held that Family Courts have exclusive jurisdiction over matters of dissolution, dower, maintenance, custody, and recovery of personal property. Civil courts cannot entertain suits falling within Family Court jurisdiction.
case_laws.summary_ur: خاندانی عدالتوں کے دائرہ اختیار سے متعلق مقدمہ۔
case_laws.headnotes: Family Court jurisdiction - WPFCA - Exclusive jurisdiction - Dissolution, dower, maintenance, custody - Civil courts barred

case_laws.title: Mst. Hina v. Imran Khan
case_laws.summary_en: Case on the right of iddat maintenance. The court held that a divorced wife is entitled to maintenance during the iddat period (approximately three months). The husband cannot avoid this obligation even if the divorce was at the wife's request through Khula. Iddat maintenance is separate from dower.
case_laws.summary_ur: عدت کے نفقے کے حق سے متعلق مقدمہ۔
case_laws.headnotes: Iddat maintenance - Divorced wife's right - Three months - Husband's obligation - Separate from dower
case_laws.relevant_statutes: ["Muslim Family Laws Ordinance", "West Pakistan Family Courts Act"]

case_laws.title: Allah Ditta v. Muhammad Aslam
case_laws.summary_en: Case regarding pre-emption rights (Shuf'a) in property sales. The Supreme Court clarified the conditions for exercising the right of pre-emption under Punjab Pre-emption Act 1913, holding that the pre-emptor must prove co-sharing, vicinage, or adjacency, and must make both Talbs.
case_laws.summary_ur: جائیداد کی فروخت میں حق شفعہ سے متعلق مقدمہ۔
case_laws.headnotes: Pre-emption - Shuf'a - Punjab Pre-emption Act 1913 - Talb-i-Mowasibat - Talb-i-Ishhad - Co-sharer rights

case_laws.title: Khan Zaman v. Gul Khan
case_laws.summary_en: Case concerning the rights of tenants under rent control laws. The court held that a tenant cannot be evicted except on grounds specified in the rent restriction law. The landlord must prove personal bonafide need or default in rent payment.
case_laws.summary_ur: کرایہ کنٹرول قوانین کے تحت کرایہ داروں کے حقوق سے متعلق مقدمہ۔
case_laws.headnotes: Tenant rights - Eviction - Rent restriction - Personal need of landlord - Default in rent

case_laws.title: Muhammad Akbar v. Fazal Din
case_laws.summary_en: Supreme Court case on benami transactions. The court held that under the Benami Transactions (Prohibition) Act 2017, any property held in a fictitious name or in the name of a benamidar is liable to confiscation by the government. The burden of proving a transaction is not benami lies on the person claiming ownership.
case_laws.summary_ur: سپریم کورٹ نے بے نامی لین دین پر فیصلہ دیا۔
case_laws.headnotes: Benami transaction - Fictitious name - Confiscation - Burden of proof - BTPA 2017

case_laws.title: Ghulam Abbas v. Muhammad Yousaf
case_laws.summary_en: Case on the validity of oral gift (Hiba) of immovable property. The court held that for a valid gift under Muslim law, three elements are essential: (i) declaration by donor, (ii) acceptance by donee, and (iii) delivery of possession. Registration is not mandatory for a valid gift under Muslim law.
case_laws.summary_ur: غیر منقولہ جائیداد کے زبانی تحفے (ہبہ) کی قانونی حیثیت سے متعلق مقدمہ۔
case_laws.headnotes: Gift - Hiba - Muslim law - Declaration, acceptance, possession - Registration not mandatory

case_laws.title: Rana Sajid v. Muhammad Pervez
case_laws.summary_en: Case on the cancellation of fraudulent sale deed. The court held that a sale deed obtained through fraud, misrepresentation, or coercion can be cancelled under Section 39 of the Specific Relief Act. The plaintiff must prove fraud with specific particulars and the suit must be filed within the limitation period.
case_laws.summary_ur: فراڈ کے ذریعے حاصل کردہ فروخت نامے کی منسوخی سے متعلق مقدمہ۔
case_laws.headnotes: Fraudulent sale deed - Cancellation - Section 39 SRA - Fraud must be proved - Limitation period
case_laws.relevant_statutes: ["Specific Relief Act", "Transfer of Property Act", "Limitation Act"]

case_laws.title: Abdul Hameed v. Government of Punjab
case_laws.summary_en: Supreme Court case on land acquisition and compensation. The court held that acquisition of private property by the government must be for a public purpose and fair compensation must be paid. The affected party has the right to challenge inadequate compensation in court.
case_laws.summary_ur: زمین کی حصولیابی اور معاوضے سے متعلق سپریم کورٹ کا مقدمہ۔
case_laws.headnotes: Land acquisition - Public purpose - Fair compensation - Article 24 Constitution - Right to challenge

case_laws.title: Nazir Ahmed v. Bashir Ahmed
case_laws.summary_en: Case on adverse possession under the Limitation Act. The court held that a person in open, continuous, and hostile possession of immovable property for 12 years acquires title by adverse possession. The possession must be without consent of the true owner and must be uninterrupted.
case_laws.summary_ur: حد تحدید ایکٹ کے تحت حصولی قبضے سے متعلق مقدمہ۔
case_laws.headnotes: Adverse possession - 12 years limitation - Open continuous hostile possession - Without owner's consent - Title acquired

case_laws.title: Tariq Hussain v. Ahmad Khan
case_laws.summary_en: Case on the specific performance of agreement to sell immovable property. The court held that specific performance can only be decreed if the agreement is valid, consideration is certain, and the plaintiff was ready and willing to perform his part. Time is not of the essence unless expressly stated.
case_laws.summary_ur: غیر منقولہ جائیداد کی فروخت کے معاہدے کی مخصوص تعمیل سے متعلق مقدمہ۔
case_laws.headnotes: Specific performance - Agreement to sell - Valid contract - Ready and willing - Time not of essence - Section 12 SRA
case_laws.relevant_statutes: ["Specific Relief Act", "Contract Act", "Transfer of Property Act"]

case_laws.title: Government of Punjab v. Muhammad Riaz
case_laws.summary_en: The Supreme Court addressed mutation (intiqal) of property in revenue records. The court held that mutation entries in revenue records are not documents of title but only reflect the factual position of possession. A person relying on mutation must also prove the underlying transaction.
case_laws.summary_ur: ریونیو ریکارڈز میں جائیداد کی انتقال سے متعلق سپریم کورٹ کا فیصلہ۔
case_laws.headnotes: Mutation - Revenue records - Not document of title - Factual position - Must prove underlying transaction

case_laws.title: Mst. Naseem v. Muhammad Arif
case_laws.summary_en: Case on partition of joint property among co-owners. The court held that every co-owner has the right to demand partition of jointly owned property. The partition must be made according to the shares of each co-owner. In case of immovable property, physical partition is preferred over partition by sale.
case_laws.summary_ur: مشترکہ مالکان کے درمیان مشترکہ جائیداد کی تقسیم سے متعلق مقدمہ۔
case_laws.headnotes: Partition - Joint property - Co-owners' right - Physical partition preferred - Shares determination
case_laws.relevant_statutes: ["Specific Relief Act", "Transfer of Property Act", "Code of Civil Procedure"]

case_laws.title: Suo Motu Case regarding Water Pollution in Balochistan
case_laws.summary_en: Landmark case on fundamental rights to clean water and environment under Article 9 and Article 14 of the Constitution. The Supreme Court held that the right to life includes the right to clean drinking water and a healthy environment.
case_laws.summary_ur: آئین کی دفعہ 9 اور 14 کے تحت صاف پانی اور ماحول کے بنیادی حقوق پر تاریخی فیصلہ۔
case_laws.headnotes: Fundamental rights - Article 9 - Right to life includes right to clean water - Article 14 - Environmental protection

case_laws.title: Supreme Court Bar Association v. Federation of Pakistan
case_laws.summary_en: Constitutional petition regarding the independence of judiciary and separation of powers. The court reaffirmed the principle that the judiciary is independent of the executive and legislature, and any attempt to undermine judicial independence is a violation of the basic structure of the Constitution.
case_laws.summary_ur: عدلیہ کی آزادی اور اختیارات کی علیحدگی سے متعلق آئینی پٹیشن۔
case_laws.headnotes: Judicial independence - Separation of powers - Basic structure doctrine - Article 175 Constitution

case_laws.title: Federation of Pakistan v. Citizens Rights Commission
case_laws.summary_en: Case on the right to access information under Article 19-A of the Constitution. The court held that every citizen has the fundamental right to access information held by public bodies. This right can only be restricted on narrow grounds of national security or cabinet confidentiality.
case_laws.summary_ur: آئین کی دفعہ 19-اے کے تحت معلومات تک رسائی کے حق سے متعلق مقدمہ۔
case_laws.headnotes: Right to information - Article 19-A - Public bodies - Narrow restrictions - National security exception
case_laws.relevant_statutes: ["Constitution of Pakistan 1973", "Right of Access to Information Act"]

case_laws.title: Mian Muhammad Nawaz Sharif v. State
case_laws.summary_en: Case on disqualification of members of Parliament under Article 62 and 63 of the Constitution. The court discussed the scope of disqualification for being not sadiq and ameen (honest and righteous) and the permanence of such disqualification.
case_laws.summary_ur: آئین کی دفعہ 62 اور 63 کے تحت اراکین پارلیمنٹ کی نااہلی سے متعلق مقدمہ۔
case_laws.headnotes: Disqualification - Article 62 - Article 63 - Sadiq and Ameen - Permanence of disqualification

case_laws.title: Government of Sindh v. Federation of Pakistan
case_laws.summary_en: Case on distribution of legislative powers between federation and provinces under the 18th Amendment. The court clarified that matters in the Federal Legislative List are exclusive federal domain, while residuary powers belong to the provinces.
case_laws.summary_ur: اٹھارہویں ترمیم کے تحت وفاق اور صوبوں کے درمیان قانون سازی کے اختیارات کی تقسیم سے متعلق مقدمہ۔
case_laws.headnotes: 18th Amendment - Federal legislative list - Provincial autonomy - Residuary powers - Distribution of powers

case_laws.title: Human Rights Commission v. Government of Pakistan
case_laws.summary_en: The Supreme Court addressed the right to education under Article 25-A of the Constitution. The court held that free and compulsory education for children aged 5-16 is a fundamental right and directed the federal and provincial governments to allocate adequate resources for its implementation.
case_laws.summary_ur: آئین کی دفعہ 25-اے کے تحت تعلیم کے حق سے متعلق سپریم کورٹ کا فیصلہ۔
case_laws.headnotes: Right to education - Article 25-A - Free compulsory education - Ages 5-16 - Government obligation - Resource allocation

case_laws.title: Dr. Mobashir Hassan v. Federation of Pakistan
case_laws.summary_en: Case on the right to freedom of expression and assembly under Articles 16 and 19 of the Constitution. The court held that peaceful protest is a fundamental right but can be reasonably restricted to prevent disruption of public order. Blanket bans on assemblies are unconstitutional.
case_laws.summary_ur: آئین کی دفعہ 16 اور 19 کے تحت اظہار رائے اور اجتماع کی آزادی سے متعلق مقدمہ۔
case_laws.headnotes: Freedom of expression - Freedom of assembly - Articles 16, 19 - Peaceful protest - Reasonable restrictions - No blanket bans

case_laws.title: Malik Brothers v. Shaheen Enterprises
case_laws.summary_en: Case on breach of commercial contract and specific performance. The court held that in commercial contracts, damages are the primary remedy and specific performance is an exceptional remedy. The party seeking specific performance must show that monetary damages are inadequate.
case_laws.summary_ur: تجارتی معاہدے کی خلاف ورزی اور مخصوص تعمیل سے متعلق مقدمہ۔
case_laws.headnotes: Breach of contract - Specific performance - Commercial contracts - Damages primary remedy - Inadequacy of damages

case_laws.title: Akhtar Industries v. Punjab Revenue Authority
case_laws.summary_en: Case on injunction in civil suits. The court discussed the three-pronged test for grant of temporary injunction: (i) prima facie case, (ii) balance of convenience, and (iii) irreparable loss. All three conditions must be satisfied for grant of interim relief.
case_laws.summary_ur: دیوانی مقدمات میں حکم امتناعی سے متعلق مقدمہ۔
case_laws.headnotes: Temporary injunction - Prima facie case - Balance of convenience - Irreparable loss - Order 39 CPC

case_laws.title: Hassan Trading Co. v. Imperial Textiles
case_laws.summary_en: Supreme Court case on the enforceability of arbitration clauses in commercial contracts. The court held that arbitration clauses are binding and courts should refer parties to arbitration unless the clause is void or inoperable. The Arbitration Act 1940 governs domestic arbitration proceedings.
case_laws.summary_ur: تجارتی معاہدوں میں ثالثی شقوں کے نفاذ سے متعلق سپریم کورٹ کا مقدمہ۔
case_laws.headnotes: Arbitration clause - Binding - Court must refer to arbitration - Arbitration Act 1940 - Void or inoperable clause

case_laws.title: Syed Enterprises v. Metro Construction
case_laws.summary_en: Case on the doctrine of estoppel and promissory estoppel in contract law. The court held that a party who makes a representation and induces another to act upon it is estopped from going back on the representation. Promissory estoppel applies even without consideration.
case_laws.summary_ur: معاہدے کے قانون میں اصول استاپل سے متعلق مقدمہ۔
case_laws.headnotes: Estoppel - Promissory estoppel - Representation - Inducement - No consideration required - Section 115 QSO

case_laws.title: Consumer Protection Council v. Pakistan Telecom
case_laws.summary_en: Case on consumer rights and deficiency in services. The court held that under the Consumer Protection Act, a consumer is entitled to compensation for defective goods or deficient services. The burden of proving that the service was not deficient lies on the service provider.
case_laws.summary_ur: صارف کے حقوق اور خدمات میں کمی سے متعلق مقدمہ۔
case_laws.headnotes: Consumer protection - Deficient services - Compensation - Burden on service provider - Consumer Protection Act

case_laws.title: Pakistan Steel Mills v. Karachi Port Trust
case_laws.summary_en: Supreme Court case on limitation in civil suits. The court reaffirmed that limitation is a jurisdictional issue and must be examined by the court suo motu. A suit barred by limitation must be dismissed regardless of the merits of the case.
case_laws.summary_ur: دیوانی مقدمات میں تحدید سے متعلق سپریم کورٹ کا مقدمہ۔
case_laws.headnotes: Limitation - Jurisdictional issue - Suo motu examination - Suit barred must be dismissed - Section 3 Limitation Act

case_laws.title: Abdul Waheed v. WAPDA
case_laws.summary_en: Case on declaration and injunction under the Specific Relief Act. The court held that a person claiming any legal right can file a declaratory suit. The plaintiff must prove his right and that the defendant denies it. Declaration without consequential relief is generally not maintainable.
case_laws.summary_ur: اعلانیہ اور حکم امتناعی سے متعلق مقدمہ۔
case_laws.headnotes: Declaratory suit - Section 42 SRA - Legal right - Denial by defendant - Consequential relief
